item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
pretty good for the most part, but there are some odd bits.

Like how would the researchers know what a bite sounds like?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think there's a workable idea in here, but a lot of attention is going to be needed to cut down the description. I'm not the only one who found it long-winded. For instance,

Noticeable patches of moss are visible over the lower trunk presumably resulting from retention of moisture. A substantial arrangement of variably sized roots, soil and small rocks is also clearly visible.

Could be written as "Moss, roots, soil and small rocks are visible on the lower trunk." and you lose nothing save some questionable tone. Of course, the whole thing could potentially be cut, because I don't think it adds anything to the article. This is the kind of editing you'll want to pursue with this, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Testing log seems a bit long to me, especially since nothing really interesting happens during them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note: I can't access imgur on my work computer, so I don't know what that .gif looks like.

I rather like the idea, but you definitely need to tighten this up. The D-class logs are largely redundant and, as moose said, the dialogue need considerable work. I'd also like to see you address what happens if they start exploring the area while they're small.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not very interesting. The writing is overall fine (minus a couple nitpicks — like the "firm" bite), although it doesn't help that the dialogue from the Class Ds is somewhat silly. Wouldn't kill the article on its own, but it doesn't help.

I did like that you went through the extra effort of creating a gif, though I am mystified as to why you'd need Level 3 clearance to view it. (You'd really only need Level 1 clearance, and why even specify that?) It also doesn't quite have the 'immense size' effect you're looking for.

This piece is nothing to be ashamed of — but it's still not working yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
███ ██████, USA

Looks like a certain town I know of…

This is ultimately uninteresting to me, and it goes on for far longer than it needs to. By Addendum 2843-A, I was very bored. By the third test log, I was wondering why there was a need to keep going. It's just not compelling to read, and I can't care or put stock into anything going on here. This isn't badly written; it just needs to grab my imagination somehow and make me care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TBH this doesn't really interest me, the concept isn't anything particularly fascinating and the test logs don't really reveal anything we didn't already know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is surprisingly well done. My only nitpick is having quotation marks around the quoted section, since the quote box means that it's quoted from somewhere, meaning that you have double quotes, but aside from that I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made it, so no copyright to worry about ;)
